feedback.text: The current digital market is bad for consumers in my perception, so i am glad there is an initiative aiming to make the market more balanced and fair. I think some of the bigger issues are: -Companies constantly pushing subscription models rather than just selling a product permanently,just to force more money out of people who depend on their products -pushing AI into everything when they don't even use it in a way that benefits people,such as AI "Art". AI should not be used to steal artists work and generate pictures or movies. AI should be used to do repetitive,menial tasks, to free up time so people can make more art and do things they enjoy. Companies who use stolen artwork to feed into their AI's for learning have to be held accountable and should have to pay the artists they stole from. -the censoring of content through payment processors such as Visa,Mastercard,Paypal or Stripe to allow people to only consume the type of content they deem moral. Payment processors should not be able to tell me how i can spend my money as long as i am purchasing legal products with it. -Companies producing games and when stopping support for said games, preventing players from using their already purchased game again. There has to be a legal way to play a game if you own it. Everything else would be to just destroy something that you own, which cannot be legal. As someone who is genuinely concerned about free speech and freedom being under attack these days, i kindly ask the european commission to keep these issues in mind and act on them.
